commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR redesigns the SetupEngine WSL gateway onboarding flow, merges permissions into the capabilities step, adds setup-scoped brand accent resources, a DEBUG-only preview route, updated wizard/progress/complete UI, and committed screenshot proof assets.

Reproducibility: yes. for the blocking review finding: the PR’s own capabilities step-3 screenshot shows the red primary action with dark text, and the changed XAML uses setup-scoped accent resources plus AccentButtonStyle. I did not run the WinUI app because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The primary repair is a narrow setup style/resource fix that automation can attempt; maintainers still own the broader UX acceptance after repair and proof.

target.title: Redesign WSL gateway onboarding (stepped, brand-themed, accessible)
target.body:
> **Draft for review/testing.** Presentation-only redesign of the WSL gateway onboarding — **no changes to connection, pairing, the install pipeline, credentials, or the gateway protocol.**

## What this is

A redesign of the OpenClaw setup/onboarding flow with a power-user lens: clearer, fewer clicks, transparent about what gets installed, brand-themed, and theme-aware (light + dark). The gateway-onboard step now reads as a vertical transcript, and the pre-install screen is a short stepped flow that ends in install.

## Try it

```powershell
./build.ps1
$env:OPENCLAW_FORCE_ONBOARDING="1"; ./run-app-local.ps1 -NoBuild -Isolated -AllowNonMaster
```

There's a **dev-only preview route** to jump straight to any screen without a real install:

```powershell
$env:OPENCLAW_SETUP_PREVIEW_PAGE="welcome"   # or: capabilities | progress | wizard | permissions | complete
```

(Env-gated — zero effect when unset.)

## Screens

### 1 · Welcome — chooser
![welcome](https://github.com/bkudiess/openclaw-windows-node/raw/155a3ccd022e3d3b80409c1e5483bc6b753a7437/docs/onboarding-redesign/00-welcome.png)

### 2 · Capabilities — stepped flow (steps accrete with checkmarks, like the onboard)
**Step 1 — what your agent can do**
![step1](https://github.com/bkudiess/openclaw-windows-node/raw/155a3ccd022e3d3b80409c1e5483bc6b753a7437/docs/onboarding-redesign/01-capabilities-step1.png)

**Step 2 — Windows permissions** (merged in from the old standalone step)
![step2](https://github.com/bkudiess/openclaw-windows-node/raw/155a3ccd022e3d3b80409c1e5483bc6b753a7437/docs/onboarding-redesign/02-capabilities-step2.png)

**Step 3 — review & install**
![step3](https://github.com/bkudiess/openclaw-windows-node/raw/155a3ccd022e3d3b80409c1e5483bc6b753a7437/docs/onboarding-redesign/03-capabilities-step3.png)

### 3 · Gateway onboard — vertical transcript
![onboard](https://github.com/bkudiess/openclaw-windows-node/raw/155a3ccd022e3d3b80409c1e5483bc6b753a7437/docs/onboarding-redesign/04-gateway-onboard.png)

#### Real end-to-end onboarding walkthrough
Captured from an actual install + gateway onboard run. The transcript accretes as each gateway question is answered, and the active step is always shown with its title.

| | |
|---|---|
| Onboard begins | ![onboard-start](https://github.com/bkudiess/openclaw-windows-node/raw/155a3ccd022e3d3b80409c1e5483bc6b753a7437/docs/onboarding-redesign/07-onboard-1-start.png) |
| Setup mode (transcript building) | ![onboard-setup-mode](https://github.com/bkudiess/openclaw-windows-node/raw/155a3ccd022e3d3b80409c1e5483bc6b753a7437/docs/onboarding-redesign/08-onboard-2-setup-mode.png) |
| Copilot auth method | ![onboard-auth](https://github.com/bkudiess/openclaw-windows-node/raw/155a3ccd022e3d3b80409c1e5483bc6b753a7437/docs/onboarding-redesign/09-onboard-3-auth-method.png) |
| Web search (with live gateway output) | ![onboard-web-search](https://github.com/bkudiess/openclaw-windows-node/raw/155a3ccd022e3d3b80409c1e5483bc6b753a7437/docs/onboarding-redesign/10-onboard-4-web-search.png) |

> Note: a follow-up commit fixes auto-scroll on steps with many options (e.g. the web-search provider list) so the step's intro/title stays in view instead of scrolling to the bottom.

### 4 · Progress — tighter steps + live-activity ledger that flows below them
![progress](https://github.com/bkudiess/openclaw-windows-node/raw/155a3ccd022e3d3b80409c1e5483bc6b753a7437/docs/onboarding-redesign/05-progress.png)

### 5 · All set
![complete](https://github.com/bkudiess/openclaw-windows-node/raw/155a3ccd022e3d3b80409c1e5483bc6b753a7437/docs/onboarding-redesign/06-complete.png)

## Notable changes

- **Welcome chooser** — Install a local gateway (WSL, recommended) vs. Connect to an existing gateway.
- **Merged permissions** — "Grant permissions" now lives inline on the capabilities screen; each OS permission row shows only when its capability is enabled. The standalone permissions page is dropped from the flow (kept for the preview route).
- **Stepped, accreting capabilities flow** — each step collapses into a green-checkmark summary above the active step, matching the gateway-onboard transcript.
- **Gateway onboard** — vertical transcript; auto-scroll keeps the active step's title in view (fixed so long option lists no longer hide the intro).
- **Progress** — step rows tightened; the live-activity ledger flows directly below the steps and opens downward instead of being pinned to the window bottom.
- **Brand-red accent**, scoped to the setup window only (light + dark). Filled controls (buttons, radios, checkboxes, toggles) use WCAG-AA reds (white text ≥ 5:1). The bright coral `#FF5C5C` failed AA at 3.03:1, so it's retained only for accent text/links.
- **Shared `SetupPermissionHelper`** used by both surfaces; removed the "what changed / how to undo" expander from the Complete page.

## Validation

- `build.ps1` ✅
- Tests: **SetupEngine 237 · Tray 958 · Shared 2049** — all green, no regressions.

Screenshots are also committed under `docs/onboarding-redesign/` (visible in the Files changed tab).
target.author: bkudiess

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
